LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS VANESSA KING AND AMARITA KING'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DISMISSAL OF DEFENDANT AMY QU LUCIER IS DENIED. UNDER CCP 473(A) A COURT MAY, "ON MOTION OF EITHER PARTY AFTER NOTICE TO THE OTHER PARTY, SET ASIDE ANY VOID JUDGMENT OR ORDER." THE COURT FINDS THERE IS NO GOOD CAUSE TO GRANT THE MOTION AND DEFENDANT WOULD SUFFER PREJUDICE SHOULD THE COURT GRANT THE MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue The Trial Date

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Dismissal Of Defendant Amy Qu Lucier
Off Calendar Jul-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Henning, of Law Offices of Brian L. Larsen, Tel. 415.398.5000, for plaintiffs;
David E. Hunter, III, of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3466, for defendants Minh Huynh.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS VANESSA KING AND AMARITA KING'S Motion To Set Aside The Dismissal Of Defendant Amy Qu Lucier is denied. Under CCP 473(a) a Court may, "on motion of either party after notice to the other party, set aside any void judgment or order." The Court finds there is no good cause to grant the motion and Defendant would suffer prejudice should the court grant the motion. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
